calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-31-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Shelton appeared by zoom for and with Christopher Hyland by zoom.
Mark Bruno (beneficiary) in propria persona.

Mr. Bruno presented oral argument to support his position with respect to the value of the car being wrong, the car not having been stored and monitored properly, and the administrator not being transparent and not having communicated with him properly. Mr. Shelton responded. The First and Final Report of Administrator on Waiver of Accounting, etc. is under submission with the intent to grant. The court will also inquire as to the method that is provided in the California Rules of Court to figure out what the total amount of the fee waiver is and deduct it from Mr. Bruno's share.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Reporter: Rocio Lopez, CSR #11194, 415-551-3717, rlopez@sftc.org.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-10-2023 Continued To Jul-31-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Shelton, via Zoom, for and with Christopher Hyland, Petitioner; Marc Bruno, in court, in propria persona, Objector; Katie Hopkins, Aaron Peskin, and Megan Kennedy, in propria persona, Interested Parties.

The court queries Mr. Bruno as to why he believes Mr. Hyland should not be appointed as Administrator. Mr. Bruno offers advocacy for his objection to Mr. Bruno being considered as Administrator. The court advises that Mr. Bruno's objections are not clear, and that appointing Mr. Hyland as Administrator would not be a detriment to the estate. Mr. Shelton offers advocacy for his client's appointment and requests that the court overrule Mr. Bruno's objections and appoint his client as Administrator or as Special Administrator to avoid any additional delays in closing the estate. Ms. Hopkins agrees with Mr. Shelton's request to appoint his client. Mr. Peskin offers advocacy for Mr. Hyland's appointment as Administrator. Ms. Hopkins offers testimony regarding the decedent's medical care and healthcare. Mr. Bruno requests leave to amend and offers testimony regarding his request. The court advises Mr. Bruno that he had ample time and opportunity to object and advises that after reviewing all papers submitted, the court does not oppose the appointment of Mr. Hyland as Administrator and overrules Mr. Bruno's objections. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration With Will Annexed. The will dated Jun-08-2019 is admitted to probate. Christopher Hyland is appointed Administrator with full authority. Bond is set at $1,103,000. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Sep-11-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
EX PARTE REQUEST TO CONTINUE HEARING RE: OPPOSITION TO PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-27-2022 Continued To Aug-08-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Shelton appeared by zoom for and with Christopher Hyland (petitioner) by zoom.
Marc Bruno (beneficiary) appeared in propria persona.

The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to August 8, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Bruno to file his objections in writing. Mr. Bruno is to have his papers filed one week prior to the hearing.

Judge: Lydia Villareal. Probate Examiner: Brandon Slykhous. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: S. Phan. Not reported.
